title: Contact Form Working in Chrome but Inconsistent in Firefox – What Should I Check?
content:
Hello everyone,

We recently received feedback from a few users who reported issues when submitting forms through Firefox, while the same forms appear to work normally in Chrome and Edge.

The website belongs to Avenue Billing Services, and the issue seems inconsistent. Some users can submit forms without any problems, while others experience submission failures or no response after clicking the submit button.

So far, we have:

• Tested the form in Chrome and Edge successfully

• Verified basic form validation settings

• Checked for server-side errors

• Cleared browser cache and cookies

Before diving deeper into debugging, I wanted to ask the community:

• What are the most common causes of form submission issues that appear only in Firefox?

• Are there any Firefox-specific settings, privacy features, or extensions that could interfere with form functionality?

• What tools or methods do you recommend for troubleshooting browser-specific issues?

Any suggestions or troubleshooting tips would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

title: Search box is broken... again
content:
Since dawn of times I'm using Firefox with a search box in the center of my Home page for new tabs and windows. It is the most important tool in my life as a web content manager. A few years ago my searches suddenly started to jump to the URL bar as soon as I started typing. An URL bar is - as the says - for URL's and it sits way at the top of my 27" and 32" screens, where my focus is not.

Luckily, this forum saved me with the about:config setting browser.newtabpage.activity-stream.improvesearch.handoffToAwesomebar > false to save my life.

A few months ago this was broken by 148.something. After about a month in hell that was repaired with a dot release. And now (151.0.3, Windows 11) it's broken again:

In normal mode it still works as expected, but the search box is not in the center anymore, it has been moved up to about 300 px from the top. Fixed, independent from window size !?! If you make the window small it moves out sight eventually.

In Private Windows it is still in the center vertically, but browser.newtabpage.activity-stream.improvesearch.handoffToAwesomebar > false does not seem to work there. As soon as I start typing it jumps to the address bar.

title: Reader View Not Displaying All Images on Pages
content:
I asked ChatGPT why images were missing and this is what it said. I'm not sure whether this is a general issue with reader view on all browsers, or just Firefox. I'm using the latest version on Windows 10.

This was my question to Chatgpt:

I have a problem with a Hubpages article when I enter reader view—not all the images are shown. I'm migrating articles to my Kilcullen science blog, but now I'm worried that if I'm transcribing from reader view, I may be missing images. Hubpages lazy loads, however I'm scrolling all the way through articles before saving to make sure all images load, but in one case when there were three consecutive images, only the last image appeared in reader view.

This was its reply: That is a real risk. Reader View is designed to extract the main content of a page, not to preserve it exactly. If HubPages is using lazy loading, galleries, image containers, or JavaScript-based image insertion, Reader View may simply omit some images even if they have loaded in the normal page.

The fact that three consecutive images became only one image in Reader View is a warning sign that Firefox's Reader View parser decided the first two weren't part of the article content or failed to recognise them.

A few things you can do:

Don't rely solely on Reader View Compare the Reader View version against the normal page. Pay particular attention to image-heavy articles. Use "Save Page As" instead After scrolling to the bottom and allowing everything to load, save the page as "Web Page, Complete". This saves the HTML and associated images locally. You can then inspect the saved page if you're unsure whether an image was missed. Check the page source Search the HTML for

title: Vimeo videos not playing on Firefox
content:
So I clicked into a video, and it did not play. Further troubleshooting showed it was a video hosted by vimeo, and youtube videos still worked. I tried on multiple vimeo hosted videos, on multiple unconnected websites (dropout, patreon, and directly on the vimeo website).

I tried the following fixes:

  • I opened it in a private window and nothing played.
  • I booted into safe mode, still nothing.
  • I turned off all of my add-ons, nope.
  • I cleared my cache and restarted, nothing.
  • I cleared my browsing data and restarted, also nothing.
  • I checked for updates: up to date.
  • I created a test profile, and it works there.
  • I opened Chrome and it worked there too.

The test profile/chrome thing suggests it's an add-on or something but the fact that I turned them all off and this still happened has me baffled.

Update: I fixed it. Was user error.
